criteria: Periodic gossip rounds execute at a configurable interval (default 200ms-1s) sending state deltas to fanout peers via UDP, with the gossip sender running as a background goroutine

Each key-value state entry carries a Lamport timestamp as the version number; receivers accept an update only if its version is strictly greater than the locally stored version, with node ID tiebreaker for equal versions
Gossip message wire format includes sender node ID, list of key-version-value triples (EntryDigest), message TTL (hop count), origin node ID, and origin clock for unique message identification
Message TTL is decremented on each forward hop; messages with TTL=0 are not forwarded further, preventing infinite propagation while allowing log_fanout(N) hop depth
Convergence test injects a state update on one node in a 10-node cluster and verifies all nodes have the update within ceil(log2(10)) * 3 gossip rounds with fanout=3, measuring rounds-to-convergence

Duplicate message detection using a bounded LRU seen-message cache (configurable size, default based on cluster size × convergence rounds) prevents redundant processing and re-forwarding of already-seen messages
Bandwidth measurement logs bytes sent per gossip round per node and verifies it scales as O(fanout * delta_size), not O(N * state_size), confirmed by tests across varying cluster sizes
State store maintains lastSent watermark to send only entries with version > lastSent, implementing delta-based dissemination instead of full-state transmission
Gossiper component integrates with peer list for random peer selection and UDP connection for message transmission, handling message encoding, sending, receiving, and forwarding
Version-based conflict resolution ensures that when two nodes concurrently write to the same key, all nodes eventually converge to the same value (higher version wins, node ID breaks ties)
Seen-message cache uses message ID derived from SHA-256 hash of origin node ID and origin clock, providing unique identification across nodes
